name: Biscotti All' Anice
recipeInstructions:
1. Place nuts in a shallow pan and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 8 to 10 minutes, or until golden brown. Let cool.
2. In a small bowl, place anise seeds and add 1 tablespoon liqueur. Infuse by heating in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 5 minutes, or place in a microwave-safe bowl and heat on high for 10 to 15 seconds until steaming. Set aside.
3. In a mixing bowl, cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs. Add anise seed mixture with liqueur and the remaining tablespoon of liqueur.
4. In a separate bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Add to the creamed mixture, mixing just until blended.
5. Cut nuts into halves or thirds and fold them into the dough. Divide the dough in half.
6. On a greased and floured baking sheet, shape each portion into two logs (about 1/2 inch high, 1-1/2 inches wide, and 14 inches long) with at least 2 inches of space between them.
7. Bake at 325°F (165°C) in the center of the oven for 25 minutes, or until lightly browned. Transfer to a rack and let cool for 5 minutes.
8. Place on a cutting board. With a serrated knife, slice at a 45-degree angle about 1/2-inch thick.
9. Arrange slices upright with 1/2 inch space between each on the baking sheet and return to the oven for an additional 10 minutes to dry slightly. Let cool on a rack.
10. Store in a tightly covered container. Makes about 3-1/2 to 4 dozen.
